divide_tiff.py.name: divide_tiff_into_quadrants
divide_tiff.py.docstring:
Divided a 3D TIFF volume into 4 spatial quadrants (2x2 grid) 
memory-efficiently by processing slice-by-slice.

visualize_napari_csv.py.docstring: Loads points from a CSV file (x,y,z,gene).

centroid_cw_processing\\utils.py.docstring:
Extracts the integer slice number from the filename.
Example: A3_ch04_slice0001.swc -> 1

Saves edited napari paths back to an SWC format.
Uses a KD-Tree to preserve original types and radiuses if nodes were slightly moved.

cloud_proofreading\\napari_stream\\unifi.docstring: Identifies connected paths (unique trees) and assigns a distinct color to each tree.

Welds boundary nodes that are within `merge_dist`.

neuron_detection\\processing.py.docstring: Runs the full pipeline on a subvolume and extracts the networkx graph.

neuron_detection\\visualization.py.docstring: Launches the Napari viewer with the detection layers.

neuron_detection\\visualize_swc_volume.p.docstring:
Parses an SWC file and returns a list of line segments (paths) 
connecting each node to its parent, formatted for Napari's Paths layer.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\clu.name: compute_invariants
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\clu.docstring: Groups individual moments into the 54 rotationally invariant shells F_nl.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: load_centroids_from_json
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring: Load cell centroids from JSON file.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: build_edge_lines
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring: Build edge line data from CW complex 1D cells for Shapes layer.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: create_proofread_widget
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring:
Create a Napari dock widget for proofreading.

Uses magicgui for widget creation if available, otherwise
falls back to a simple QWidget.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: _do_proofread_action
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring: Execute a proofreading action on the currently selected point.

neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.name: launch_napari_viewer
neuron_processing\\zernike_pipeline\\nap.docstring:
Launch the Napari viewer with all layers and proofreading.

Parameters
----------
volume_path : str or None
    Path to raw DAPI TIFF volume.
labels_path : str or None
    Path to cell labels TIFF.
centroids_path : str or None
    Path to centroids JSON.
cw_path : str or None
    Path to CW complex JSON.
output_dir : str
    Output directory for proofread exports.

proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.name: read_swc
proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.docstring:
Reads an SWC file and returns a pandas DataFrame.
Format: id type x y z r p

proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.name: save_swc
proofreading\\napari_proofreader.py.docstring:
Saves centroids coordinates (in physical units or pixel units, depending on scale) back to SWC.
Here coords are in physical units (Z, Y, X) because Napari displays/edits them scaled, 
but let's write them matching the original SWC structure.
coords: numpy array of shape (N, 3) representing (z, y, x) in physical space.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\batch_.name: compute_invariants
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\batch_.docstring: Groups individual moments into the 54 rotationally invariant shells F_nl.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.name: extract
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.docstring: Extract the shape descriptors INSTANTANEOUSLY via a single matrix multiplication.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.name: reconstruct
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\build_.docstring: Reconstruct the 3D volume INSTANTANEOUSLY via Matrix cross-multiplication.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_3d_basis_physical
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the full 3D Zernike polynomial Z_{nl}^m on physical coordinates.

Parameters
----------
n : int
    Degree (n >= 0).
l : int
    Order (0 <= l <= n, and n - l is even).
m : int
    Azimuthal index (-l <= m <= l).
x_phys, y_phys, z_phys : np.ndarray
    Physical Cartesian coordinates.
R_max : float
    Physical radius of the bounding sphere.
    
Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Complex values of the Zernike polynomial at each point, normalized such that
    the volumetric integral of the squared magnitude over the sphere evaluates to 1.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: _q_coeff
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Calculate the coefficient Q_{nl
u} for the 3D Zernike radial polynomial.
    Uses standard python math since n, l, nu are small scalars.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_radial_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the 3D Zernike radial polynomial R_{nl}(r) on the GPU.
r is a cupy array.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_spherical_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the Spherical Harmonic Y_l^m(theta, phi) on the GPU.
theta and phi are cupy arrays.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_3d_basis_physical_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring: Evaluate the full 3D Zernike polynomial Z_{nl}^m on physical coordinates using GPU.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_3d_basis_precomputed_gpu
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the Zernike polynomial using highly-optimized precomputed spherical coordinates.
Optionally accepts a precomputed radial array R_cached to avoid redundant recalculation.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: _q_coeff
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Calculate the coefficient Q_{nl
u} for the 3D Zernike radial polynomial.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_radial
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the 3D Zernike radial polynomial R_{nl}(r).

Parameters
----------
n : int
    Degree of the polynomial (n >= 0).
l : int
    Order of the polynomial (0 <= l <= n, and n - l is even).
r : np.ndarray
    Radial distances (usually r <= 1.0).
    
Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Evaluated radial polynomial values at r.

zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.name: zernike_spherical
zernike_feature_pipeline_package\\zernik.docstring:
Evaluate the Spherical Harmonic Y_l^m(theta, phi).

Parameters
----------
l : int
    Degree of the harmonic (l >= 0).
m : int
    Order of the harmonic (-l <= m <= l).
theta : np.ndarray
    Polar angle in radians [0, pi].
phi : np.ndarray
    Azimuthal angle in radians [0, 2*pi].
    
Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Evaluated complex spherical harmonic values.
    
Notes
-----
In scipy's sph_harm signature, the order of angles is (m, n, theta, phi) 
where theta is the azimuthal angle [0, 2pi] and phi is the polar angle [0, pi].
We wrap this to use standard physics notation where theta is polar [0, pi]
and phi is azimuthal [0, 2pi].
